english_name: The Second Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: Statui autem hoc ipsum apud me, ne iterum in tristitia venirem ad vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔκρινα δὲ ἐμαυτῷ τοῦτο τὸ μὴ πάλιν ἐλθεῖν ἐν λύπῃ πρὸς ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: But I determined this with myself, that I would not come again to you in heaviness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si enim ego contristo vos: et quis est, qui me lætificet, nisi qui contristatur ex me?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ ἐγὼ λυπῶ ὑμᾶς καὶ τίς ἐστιν ὁ εὐφραίνων με εἰ μὴ ὁ λυπούμενος ἐξ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: For if I make you sorry, who is he then that maketh me glad, but the same which is made sorry by me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hoc ipsum scripsi vobis, ut non cum venero, tristitiam super tristitiam habeam, de quibus oportuerat me gaudere: confidens in omnibus vobis, quia meum gaudium, omnium vestrum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔγραψα ὑμῖν τοῦτο αὐτὸ ἵνα μὴ ἐλθὼν λύπην ἔχω ἀφ᾽ ὧν ἔδει με χαίρειν πεποιθὼς ἐπὶ πάντας ὑμᾶς ὅτι ἡ ἐμὴ χαρὰ πάντων ὑμῶν ἐστιν
verses.king_james: And I wrote this same unto you, lest, when I came, I should have sorrow from them of whom I ought to rejoice; having confidence in you all, that my joy is *the joy* of you all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam ex multa tribulatione et angustia cordis scripsi vobis per multas lacrimas: non ut contristemini, sed ut sciatis, quam caritatem habeam abundantius in vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκ γὰρ πολλῆς θλίψεως καὶ συνοχῆς καρδίας ἔγραψα ὑμῖν διὰ πολλῶν δακρύων οὐχ ἵνα λυπηθῆτε ἀλλὰ τὴν ἀγάπην ἵνα γνῶτε ἣν ἔχω περισσοτέρως εἰς ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote unto you with many tears; not that ye should be grieved, but that ye might know the love which I have more abundantly unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis autem contristavit, non me contristavit: sed ex parte, ut non onerem omnes vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δέ τις λελύπηκεν οὐκ ἐμὲ λελύπηκεν ἀλλ᾽ ἀπὸ μέρους ἵνα μὴ ἐπιβαρῶ πάντας ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: But if any have caused grief, he hath not grieved me, but in part: that I may not overcharge you all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sufficit illi, qui ejusmodi est, objurgatio hæc, quæ fit a pluribus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἱκανὸν τῷ τοιούτῳ ἡ ἐπιτιμία αὕτη ἡ ὑπὸ τῶν πλειόνων
verses.king_james: Sufficient to such a man *is* this punishment, which *was inflicted* of many.

verses.latin_vulgate: ita ut e contrario magis donetis, et consolemini, ne forte abundantiori tristitia absorbeatur qui ejusmodi est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥστε τοὐναντίον μᾶλλον ὑμᾶς χαρίσασθαι καὶ παρακαλέσαι μήπως τῇ περισσοτέρᾳ λύπῃ καταποθῇ ὁ τοιοῦτος
verses.king_james: So that contrariwise ye *ought* rather to forgive *him*, and comfort *him*, lest perhaps such a one should be swallowed up with overmuch sorrow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod obsecro vos, ut confirmetis in illum caritatem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ παρακαλῶ ὑμᾶς κυρῶσαι εἰς αὐτὸν ἀγάπην
verses.king_james: Wherefore I beseech you that ye would confirm *your* love toward him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo enim et scripsi, ut cognoscam experimentum vestrum, an in omnibus obedientes sitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς τοῦτο γὰρ καὶ ἔγραψα ἵνα γνῶ τὴν δοκιμὴν ὑμῶν εἰ εἰς πάντα ὑπήκοοί ἐστε
verses.king_james: For to this end also did I write, that I might know the proof of you, whether ye be obedient in all things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui autem aliquid donastis, et ego: nam et ego quod donavi, si quid donavi, propter vos in persona Christi,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ᾧ δέ τι χαρίζεσθε καὶ ἐγὼ καὶ γὰρ ἐγώ εἴ τι κεχάρισμαι ᾧ κεχάρισμαι δι᾽ ὑμᾶς ἐν προσώπῳ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: To whom ye forgive any thing, I *forgive* also: for if I forgave any thing, to whom i forgave *it*, for your sakes *forgave I it* in the person of Christ;

verses.latin_vulgate: ut non circumveniamur a Satana: non enim ignoramus cogitationes ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα μὴ πλεονεκτηθῶμεν ὑπὸ τοῦ σατανᾶ οὐ γὰρ αὐτοῦ τὰ νοήματα ἀγνοοῦμεν
verses.king_james: Lest Satan should get an advantage of us: for we are not ignorant of his devices.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum venissem autem Troadem propter Evangelium Christi, et ostium mihi apertum esset in Domino,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλθὼν δὲ εἰς τὴν τρῳάδα εἰς τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τοῦ χριστοῦ καὶ θύρας μοι ἀνεῳγμένης ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: Furthermore, when I came to Troas to *preach* Christ's gospel, and a door was opened unto me of the Lord,

verses.latin_vulgate: non habui requiem spiritui meo, eo quod non invenerim Titum fratrem meum, sed valefaciens eis, profectus sum in Macedoniam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἔσχηκα ἄνεσιν τῷ πνεύματί μου τῷ μὴ εὑρεῖν με τίτον τὸν ἀδελφόν μου ἀλλὰ ἀποταξάμενος αὐτοῖς ἐξῆλθον εἰς Μακεδονίαν
verses.king_james: I had no rest in my spirit, because I found not Titus my brother: but taking my leave of them, I went from thence into Macedonia.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deo autem gratias, qui semper triumphat nos in Christo Jesu, et odorem notitiæ suæ manifestat per nos in omni loco:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῷ δὲ θεῷ χάρις τῷ πάντοτε θριαμβεύοντι ἡμᾶς ἐν τῷ χριστῷ καὶ τὴν ὀσμὴν τῆς γνώσεως αὐτοῦ φανεροῦντι δι᾽ ἡμῶν ἐν παντὶ τόπῳ
verses.king_james: Now thanks *be* unto God, which always causeth us to triumph in Christ, and maketh manifest the savour of his knowledge by us in every place.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia Christi bonus odor sumus Deo in iis qui salvi fiunt, et in his qui pereunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι χριστοῦ εὐωδία ἐσμὲν τῷ θεῷ ἐν τοῖς σῳζομένοις καὶ ἐν τοῖς ἀπολλυμένοις
verses.king_james: For we are unto God a sweet savour of Christ, in them that are saved, and in them that perish:

verses.latin_vulgate: aliis quidem odor mortis in mortem: aliis autem odor vitæ in vitam. Et ad hæc quis tam idoneus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἷς μὲν ὀσμὴ θανάτου εἰς θάνατον οἷς δὲ ὀσμὴ ζωῆς εἰς ζωήν καὶ πρὸς ταῦτα τίς ἱκανός
verses.king_james: To the one *we are* the savour of death unto death; and to the other the savour of life unto life. And who *is* sufficient for these things?

verses.latin_vulgate: non enim sumus sicut plurimi, adulterantes verbum Dei, sed ex sinceritate, sed sicut ex Deo, coram Deo, in Christo loquimur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γάρ ἐσμεν ὡς οἱ πολλοὶ καπηλεύοντες τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ ἀλλ᾽ ὡς ἐξ εἰλικρινείας ἀλλ᾽ ὡς ἐκ θεοῦ κατενώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ ἐν χριστῷ λαλοῦμεν
verses.king_james: For we are not as many, which corrupt the word of God: but as of sincerity, but as of God, in the sight of God speak we in Christ.
